# escape-html

[![NPM Version][npm-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][downloads-image]][downloads-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Escape string for use in HTML.

## Installation

This is a [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/en/) module available through the
[npm registry](https://www.npmjs.com/). Installation is done using the
[`npm install` command](https://docs.npmjs.com/getting-started/installing-npm-packages-locally):

```bash
$ npm install escape-html
```

## API

```js
var escape = require('escape-html')
var html = escape('foo & bar')
// -> "foo &amp; bar"
```

### escape(string)

Escape special characters in the given string of text, such that it can be
interpolated in HTML content.

This function will escape the following characters: `"`, `'`, `&`, `<`, and
`>`. This should be sufficient for escaping string for inclusion in HTML
content in most contexts.

**Note** that this function does not escape other characters that may need to
be escaped in some HTML contexts such as U+2028, U+2029, and characters that
can potentially lead to XSS vulnerabilities when placed within certain
HTML tags such as `<script>` or `<style>`. It is not a complete solution
for escaping HTML.

## Examples

### Escaping a string for HTML

```js
var escape = require('escape-html')
var html = escape('foo & bar')
// -> "foo &amp; bar"
```

### Escaping user input for HTML

```js
var escape = require('escape-html')
var express = require('express')

var app = express()

app.get('/user', function (req, res) {
  // Get user input from query string
  var name = req.query.name || 'Anonymous'
  
  // Escape the user input for HTML
  var escapedName = escape(name)
  
  // Send HTML response with escaped content
  res.send('<h1>Hello, ' + escapedName + '!</h1>')
})
```

## Security Considerations

This module is designed to escape text for inclusion in HTML content. It is
not designed to escape for other contexts such as:

- JavaScript (e.g., content in `<script>` tags)
- CSS (e.g., content in `<style>` tags or style attributes)
- URL parameters (e.g., content in `href` or `src` attributes)

For these contexts, additional escaping mechanisms should be used that are
specific to those contexts.

## Character Escaping

The following table shows the characters that are escaped by this module and
their corresponding HTML entity:

| Character | Escaped As |
|-----------|------------|
| `"`      | `&quot;`   |
| `'`      | `&#39;`    |
| `&`      | `&amp;`    |
| `<`      | `&lt;`     |
| `>`      | `&gt;`     |

## Use with Express.js

This module is used internally by Express.js for escaping HTML content. When
using Express.js, you typically don't need to require this module directly as
Express provides methods that use it:

```js
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

app.get('/', function (req, res) {
  // Express automatically escapes content when using res.render with a template engine
  res.render('index', { name: 'User & Input' })
})
```
